rationale: The hyp_abstract's engine is a moment-driven threshold analysis: generating-function/branching-process recursions on a heavy-tailed connectivity distribution showing the critical point collapses (to zero conversion) once the distribution's second moment diverges. The candidate runs the direct analogue of that same machinery — an exact critical-temperature calculation on a network with arbitrary degree distribution P(k), showing T_c diverges/the transition becomes infinite-order precisely when the second moment of P(k) diverges — the same second-moment-divergence-destroys-the-threshold structure, so method_coherence is TRUE. However the candidate's object is an Ising ferromagnet on a heterogeneous-degree network, i.e. still a statistical-physics-of-complex-networks critical-phenomena study like the query's own epidemic-threshold paper (same substrate: a scale-free/arbitrary-degree network examined for anomalous critical behavior), not any move toward the sought polymer-chemistry gelation domain (no monomers, polymerization, or chemistry appear at all) — so object_difference is FALSE.
